title: ax25.py: callsign_to_bytes allows non-ASCII characters that produce invalid frames
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/kiss/ax25.py
Lines: 32-71

The callsign_to_bytes function handles non-alphanumeric characters but not non-ASCII:

```python
for c in call:
    if c.isalnum():
        result.append(ord(c) << 1)
    else:
        result.append(ord(' ') << 1)  # Replace with space
```

If `call` contains non-ASCII characters (e.g., 'é'), `c.isalnum()` returns True but `ord(c) << 1` produces a value > 255, which will be silently truncated when converted to bytes, producing garbage.

Example: 'é' (e-acute) -> ord=233 -> 233 << 1 = 466 -> truncated to 210 in byte

Impact: Malformed AX.25 frames when callsigns contain non-ASCII characters.

Fix: Add ASCII validation:
```python
for c in call:
    if c.isalnum() and ord(c) < 128:
        result.append(ord(c) << 1)
    else:
        result.append(ord(' ') << 1)
```

Or validate at input and reject non-ASCII callsigns.
close_reason: Fixed non-ASCII callsign handling in callsign_to_bytes by adding ord(c) < 128 check. Tests pass. Commit c807f25f3
